Coronary Artery Risk Development in Young Adults

About the dataset

Primary description: Cardiovascular risk in young adults

The Coronary Artery Risk Development in Young Adults (CARDIA) is an ongoing longitudinal study of the development and trajectories of cardiovascular disease in a bi-racial population-based cohort. Data collection began in 1985 with follow-up visits in 5-year intervals, at four sites in the USA. Brain MRI began in CARDIA year 25 and continued in year 30 and year 35, with a subset of less than 1,000 of over 5,000 subjects participating in the brain MRI protocol. Non-imaging data from the CARDIA study is available through the NHLBI’s BIOLINCC [https://biolincc.nhlbi.nih.gov/studies/cardia/].
Study Coronary Artery Risk Development in Young Adults
Study Website CARDIA
Data Descriptor Paper https://doi.org/10.1161/JAHA.117.006750
DUA https://biolincc.nhlbi.nih.gov/studies/cardia/
Country/Region USA
Disease Tag cardiovascular
Age (mean ± SD)
(Baseline) 		51.2 ± 4.0
Age Range
(Baseline) 		42-61
% Female 52.8

fMRIPrep (23.1.3)
(1164/2293 complete)
/cbica/projects/cardia/Pipelines/CARDIA_fMRI_2025/Protocols
/opt/conda/envs/fmriprep/bin/fmriprep \ /cbica/comp_space/cardia/Data/BIDS/ \ /cbica/projects/cardia/Pipelines/CARDIA_fMRI_2025/Protocols/b00 \ participant \ --participant_label sub-10005 \ --fs-license-file /cbica/projects/cardia/Pipelines/CARDIA_fMRI_2025/license.txt \ --work /cbica/comp_space/cardia/ \ --fs-no-reconall \ --use-syn-sdc warn \ --output-spaces MNI152NLin2009cAsym:res-2 \ --debug all
XCP-D (0.7.1rc6)
(692/2293 complete)
/cbica/projects/cardia/Pipelines/CARDIA_fMRI_2025/Protocols/XCP
/usr/local/miniconda/bin/xcp_d \ /cbica/projects/cardia/Pipelines/CARDIA_fMRI_2025/Protocols/b00 \ /cbica/projects/cardia/Pipelines/CARDIA_fMRI_2025/Protocols/XCP/b00 \ participant \ --participant_label 10386 \ --input-type fmriprep \ -p 36P \ --work-dir /cbica/comp_space/cardia \ --motion-filter-type lp \ --band-stop-min 6 \ --motion-filter-order 4 \ --despike \ --fd-thresh 0 \ --lower-bpf 0.01 \ --upper-bpf 0.08 \ --bpf-order 2 \ --head-radius auto \ --dummy-scans auto \ --min-coverage 0.5 \ --smoothing 0 \ --fs-license-file /cbica/projects/cardia/Pipelines/CARDIA_fMRI_2025/license.txt

This study: subset with df[df.Study == 'CARDIA']


Funding
The CARDIA (Coronary Artery Risk Development in Young Adults) study — including its brain MRI substudy — is primarily supported by the U.S. National Institutes of Health (NIH), specifically through the National Heart, Lung, and Blood Institute (NHLBI).

Get Support
